text.title: structured-tool-errors Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns that defeat the deterministic dispatch the closed-enum envelope is supposed to give.

antipattern.symptom: Tool returns `code: "429"` or `code: "500"` — transport-layer codes used as semantic codes.
antipattern.root-cause: Author copied the HTTP status from the upstream response without translating.
antipattern.fix: Map to a semantic UPPER_SNAKE code: `429 → UPSTREAM_RATE_LIMITED`, `500 → UPSTREAM_INTERNAL_ERROR`, `401 → AUTH_REQUIRED`. Status codes describe transport; semantic codes drive the loop.

antipattern.symptom: `details.raw_response` contains a full HTML error page or Python stack trace; model context blows past 30K tokens on a single failure.
antipattern.root-cause: Author defensive-copied the upstream body into `details` "just in case".
antipattern.fix: Sanitise: keep `upstream_status: int`, `upstream_request_id: str`, and a sub-100-char excerpt. Drop stack frames, file paths, HTML.

antipattern.symptom: Runner returns the error envelope to the model on every hint and lets the model decide whether to retry. Token spend balloons; loop runs over `--max-turns`.
antipattern.root-cause: Author skipped the runner hint→action map; "the LLM will figure it out" pattern.
antipattern.fix: Wire the closed enum to deterministic runner actions per `templates/runner_policy.yaml`. The model only sees the error when the hint is `CHECK_INPUT` / `TRY_ALTERNATIVE`.

antipattern.symptom: Tool emits `RETRY_LATER` for unrecoverable failures (e.g., `404 Not Found`); runner retries N times then gives up after burning the budget.
antipattern.root-cause: Author chose the safest-sounding hint instead of the correct one.
antipattern.fix: Map by failure category, not by safety: `404` → `REPORT_TO_USER`; only true transient failures (rate-limit, 503, network) get `RETRY_LATER`.

antipattern.symptom: `recoveryHint: "Try again later or contact support."` — natural-language string instead of an enum value.
antipattern.fix: Use exactly one of the five closed values. The string the model sees is irrelevant; the runner reads the enum. Put user-facing copy in `message`.
